channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
If you’re a fan of popular free-to-play gacha games, then we probably don’t need to remind you that Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 is coming. The new update is a big addition for 2026, and this hub has the latest on its release date and start times.

Does anyone do F2P gacha games better than miHoYo? Genshin Impact could have been a one-off, but the developer fired off Honkai Star Rail and Zenless Zone Zero in quick succession to keep the momentum going. The latter is what we’re focusing on, in particular, the Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7.

When Is Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

You download and install the Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 from March 24.

HoYoverse has confirmed this as the release date, and ‘Champions Never Fall to the Past,’ will include the following: The Hollow Champion competition, new allies in Nangong Yu and Cissia joining the fray, themed activities at the Simmer Hotpot Restaurant, and Simulated Battle Trial tweaks and additions.

  • More Insider Gaming: Honkai Star Rail 4.1 Update Release Date & Start Times

There’s a lot to look forward to, and if version 2.7 is derailed, Insider Gaming will let you know about it.

Zenless Zone Zero 2.7 Update Release Times

Based on previous Zenless Zone Zero updates, you should expect 2.7 to go live from 7 PM PT on March 24.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising: Release Date & Card List

Whether you’re based elsewhere in America, you live in Europe, or perhaps you’re based slightly further south in Australasia, the table below covers many of the biggest time zones in the world.

Check out when the 2.7 Update drops.

Are you looking forward to being in the zone for the new Zenless Zone Zero content? If you want to talk about the game or anything else in general with the IG community, check out the free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, read about Warner Bros. Montréal being hit by layoffs, and Resident Evil Requiem selling 6 million copies to become the fastest-selling in the series.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Saudi Investment Firm Acquires Major Stake in Capcom
item.content:encoded:
An investment fund from Saudi Arabia, Electronic Gaming Development Company, has acquired a 5% stake in Capcom.

Interest from Saudi investment firms in the gaming industry has been increasing tremendously over recent years. It was recently revealed that the Saudi Public Investment Fund could become the majority owner of EA if its proposed takeover deal is completed. Now, Capcom is receiving a significant investment from another Saudi-based entity.

Saudi’s EGDC Owns a 5% Stake in Capcom

A report from GameBiz (via Automaton) reveals that the Saudi investment firm EGDC has acquired a 5.03% stake in Capcom by purchasing 26,788,500 shares. Through the acquisition, the company aims to earn profits from increases in stock prices or dividends. Saudi Arabian Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman owns EGDC through the MiSK Foundation, and the company owns 100% of SNK Corporation, the maker of Fatal Fury and King of Fighters.

EGDC’s investment is not the only Saudi investment in Capcom; the Saudi Public Investment Fund also holds a similar stake. A report from Arab News reveals that PIF also purchased 5% stakes in Capcom and Nexon Co. for $1.2 billion back in 2022.

Talking about Saudi investments in gaming, last month it was reported that Saudi’s Savvy Group could reportedly pay $7 billion to purchase Moonton from ByteDance, securing the Mobile Legends: Bang Bang label.

In other news, Capcom’s Resident Evil Requiem has already sold over 6 million copies, making it the fastest-selling game in the series. What are your thoughts on Saudi Arabia’s Electronic Gaming Development Company acquiring a 5% stake in Capcom? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Warner Bros. Montreal being affected by multiple layoffs, which could impact future releases. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Team Yandex Triumph at PGL Wallachia Season 7 as Liquid Endure 12-Hour Day
item.content:encoded:
Team Yandex claimed the PGL Wallachia Season 7 trophy following a marathon day of DotA 2, which saw Liquid play almost eight-hours of DotA across a 12-hour period.

Liquid set a record day of endurance at PGL Wallachia Season 7

The last day of the event was a marathon day for Liquid, playing the Lower Final Bo3 and the Grand Final Bo5, but even that does not tell the whole story.

BetBoom and Liquid’s Lower Bracket Final was the second-longest Bo3 in competitive DotA 2 history, topping out at 3 hours and 45 minutes, with the longest contest being the 1 hour 23 minute Game 2.

Following the gruelling contest, the team was shortly back in the server to play their 4-hour and 10-minute Grand Final against Yandex, racking up 7 hours and 55 minutes of in-server action across a 12 hour period.

While Liquid and BetBoom’s Lower Final was exceedingly long for a Bo3, it is a full hour shorter than the Bo3 record held by Cloud9 and ScaryFaceZ. The 4-hour and 48-minute contest was held at StarLadder StarSeries Season 12: Europe and CIS in 2015.

Yandex secure PGL Wallachia Season 7 trophy with a stand-in

Going into the tournament, it was unclear whether Team Yandex would be competitive, considering the absence of Offlaner Evgeniy ‘Noticed’ Ignatenko due to Visa issues. Their first series, a 0-2 loss to Heroic, lowered expectations further.

However, the Russian roster warmed up into the competition nicely after defeating PGL Wallachia Season 6 winners MOUZ 2-1 to kickstart the event with benched Position 3 Parivision player Dmitry ‘DM’ Dorokhin.

Yandex hardly looked back from that point, dropping only two more games before Sunday’s Grand Final, defeating Falcons, Parivision, Aurora, Liquid, and BetBoom in the process.

Sunday’s Grand Final looked to again be a straightforward path for Yandex as they rushed to two 37-minute opening victories behind strong performances by Ilya ‘CHIRA_JUNIOR’ Chirtsov and Alimzhan ‘watson’ Islambekov on Ember Spirit and Ursa.

Liquid fought back, showing incredible resilience after their marathon Bo3 to take a 72-minute contest in Game 3, which saw Michael ‘miCKe’ Vu wrestle the contest in Liquid’s favour.

Suddenly, Liquid were threatening the reverse sweep as Yandex fell completely flat in Game 4, securing only a single kill in the entire 34-minute game.

However, Yandex were not to be denied in Game 5, keeping their composure as errors crept in to an exhausted Liquid in a climactic 56-minute victory that saw Yandex win with DM as stand-in.

Can Yandex continue its winning ways with their full roster? Discuss that, and more, on the new esports channel of Insider Gaming’s Discord!

For more Valve news, see Valve’s response to its recent loot box lawsuits.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Honkai Star Rail 4.1 Update Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
If you’re looking forward to the Honkai Star Rail 4.1 Update, then you need to note down the full release date and stat times for the ‘Unraveled for Daybreak’ update.

The third anniversary of Honkai Star Rail approaches. miHoYo seems to catch lightning in a bottle with every one of its games, but there’s something different about Honkai Star Rail. It still retains millions of players monthly, and the 4.1 Update brings new content.

When Is Honkai Star Rail 4.1 Update Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

The Honkai Star Rail 4.1 Update is due for release on March 25, and launches simultaneously on PS5, PC, iOS, and Android.

A lot is on the way. Not only will you be able to get 5-star characters in Hyacine and Boothill, but Ashveil will also become a playable companion! This barely even dives into what the Update 4.1 content holds, and even though this is the latest of the monthly (or close enough) updates, there’s always plenty to keep you going.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising: Release Date & Card List

If miHoYo announces any delays to the new season, Insider Gaming will be the first to let you know. So keep checking back for the latest details on Honkai Star Rail’s 4.1 Update.

Honkai Star Rail 4.1 Update Release Times

Expect the Honkai Star Rail 4.1 Update to come out at the same time as other updates, 7 PM PT first chronologically, with other regions to follow.

The good thing about previous Honkai content updates is that they generally all release at the same time as they have done before!

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Register For Fortnite Save The World

Again, this could change, but it’s not expected to. For now, here are the expected start times for Update 4.1 across the world.

Check out the trailer for Honkai Star Rail’s 4.1 Update below.

Are you looking forward to 4.1? What needs to be changed or added for future content? Let everyone know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Read about Warner Bros. Montréal being hit by layoffs for more gaming news.
